documents.title: Getting Started with Programming
documents.content: Getting Started with Programming Programming is the art of writing instructions for computers to follow. Basic Concepts The first step in learning programming is understanding fundamental concepts like variables, loops, and conditionals. Variables Variables are containers for storing data values. Loops Loops allow you to repeat a block of code multiple times. Next Steps Once you master the basics, explore object-oriented programming and data structures.

documents.title: HTML Document Example
documents.content: HTML Document Example This is a sample HTML document that demonstrates how HTML content is rendered within the WebDoc site layout. HTML documents allow for more complex custom formatting. Styled Content Note: HTML documents are sanitized for security while preserving legitimate formatting elements. Tables Feature Markdown HTML Custom styling Limited Full control Ease of writing Simple Verbose Complex layouts Not supported Fully supported Lists Ordered List Read the documentation Choose your content format (Markdown or HTML) Create your document with appropriate frontmatter Place it in the correct locale and category directory Unordered List Supports bold and italic text Supports hyperlinks Supports inline code formatting Supports nested elements Blockquote HTML documents provide maximum flexibility for content authors who need precise control over formatting and layout.
